title: Somalia Conflict Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: Southwest State's parliament re-elected President Laftagareen by 66–13 votes on March 28, cementing a tripartite opposition bloc — Southwest State, Puntland, and Jubbaland — against Mogadishu's March 2026 constitutional amendments; on March 25, the Federal Government bypassed Southwest State by unilaterally appointing Bakool officials and the JSP Secretary General resigned accusing President Mohamud of running a 'one-man show.' UNICEF Executive Director Catherine Russell visited Dollow on March 27 and issued an emergency warning that 6.5 million Somalis face acute hunger — up 1.7 million since January — with the 2025 Deyr cereal harvest 83% below the 30-year average and Iran-Israel war supply chain disruptions threatening to raise humanitarian transit costs 30–60%. On the military front, elite Gorgor Commandos conducted operations in War-Iise district (Middle Shabelle) on March 24, while SNA and AUSSOM/UPDF forces killed 30+ al-Shabaab fighters in a joint operation at Bulunagaad Base in Lower Shabelle as Operation Rolling Thunder continued its Ramadan offensive.

title: Somalia Conflict Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Somalia's Federal Government launched 'Operation Rolling Thunder' on March 1 alongside Ugandan AUSSOM troops in Lower Shabelle, targeting al-Shabaab strongholds and supply routes with early territorial gains. A deepening political crisis erupted in mid-March when Somalia's parliament — boycotted by the opposition — voted to extend presidential and legislative terms from four to five years via constitutional amendment, prompting Southwest State to formally suspend relations with Mogadishu and accuse the federal government of a 'blatant invasion' involving SNA troop deployments and a civilian air-traffic blockade on Baidoa. US AFRICOM continued its intensive airstrike campaign, conducting at least 47 strikes in Somalia in 2026, including a March 19 strike 80 km northeast of Jamaame targeting al-Shabaab and multiple March strikes against ISIS-Somalia in Puntland. Al-Shabaab released a propaganda video on March 22 celebrating its October 2025 Godka Jilacow prison raid, signalling continued information operations despite battlefield pressure.
